mod.name: Closed Loop Ecology
mod.description:
Realistic carbon and nitrogen cycles for Space Haven.
Vanilla's life-support economy has two problems: - Composting biomass loses 87% of mass to the void (1 biomass → 0.017 fertilizer + 0.10 water + 0.017 carbon, total ~0.13 units recovered). - Carbon is mineable but gated at Mining skill level 7, on par with Noble Metals — unjustified given carbon's natural abundance in space. - Chemicals (fertilizer precursor) is locked behind Rot, which only comes from combat/corpses. Peaceful playthroughs can't sustain the food cycle.
This mod closes the loops with five targeted patches:
1. **Carbon mining accessible** — drops Mining requirement from level 7 → 2 (matches Base Metals). Raw Chemicals from 6 → 3. Carbonaceous chondrite asteroids should be early-game minable.
2. **Composter retains realistic carbon** — bumps biomass→carbon and produce→carbon outputs ~6× (from 0.017 to ~0.10). Matches real biochar yields of 30-70% carbon retention.
3. **CO2 scrubber actually captures carbon** — boosts atmospheric carbon recovery 10×. Crew respiration is no longer just a vent loss.
4. **New industrial chemicals synthesis** — adds a recipe to the existing Chemical Refinery: 1 Carbon + 3 Water → 1 Chemicals + 1 Oxygen. Real-world analog: Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. Closes the food cycle without combat dependency.
5. **New biomass pyrolysis recipe** — 2 Biomass → 1 Carbon + 50 CO2 (industrial charcoal production). Bypasses composter for high-throughput carbon refining.
After install, a peaceful 5-crew ship can sustain its fertilizer cycle without trading or hunting, while carbon becomes a meaningful mid-game resource instead of a useless byproduct.
mod.author: mingc
